fullArticle:
Nike full-year revenues were down 2% in constant currency for fiscal 2026 to $46.4 billion, the brand said on Tuesday. For the fourth quarter of fiscal 2026, sales were down 1% year-on-year to $11 billion. Full-year results beat analyst expectations of about $46.27 billion, reflecting steps towards Nike’s much-needed turnaround. Shares fell 3.4% after markets closed.

“I’m proud of the progress our team made in fiscal year 2026. When I look back at where we started the year and where we are today, it’s clear that we’re building a much stronger foundation for our company,” president and CEO Elliott Hill, who is approaching two years in the role, said on Tuesday’s earnings call. “Through our Win Now priorities, we're elevating the fundamentals of our business across our team culture, innovat...
